summary: Ukrainian air defenses reported shooting down 217 hostile drones while repelling an Iskander‑M ballistic missile strike from Kursk. Russian forces captured several villages in Dnipropetrovsk and Kharkiv regions. Sweden pledged delivery of 16 Gripen fighters and Ukraine plans to acquire up to 20 more.
key_points: Ukrainian air defence downed 217 Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and other drones across north, south and east; 14 locations hit by ballistic missile and UAVs (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
Russia seized Lesnoe and Novopodgorodne in Dnipropetrovsk and Karachne and Budarki in Kharkiv (Source: @tass_agency/378059).
Sweden will transfer 16 JAS‑39C/D Gripen fighters to Ukraine, with deliveries expected early 2027 (Source: @UkraineNow/81512).
UAVs launched from the Black Sea flew toward southern Mykolaiv, Odesa and the Black Sea coast, indicating maritime‑origin drone activity (Source: @kpszsu/63170, @kpszsu/63168, @kpszsu/63191).
Guided aviation bombs were dropped on Zaporizhzhia region by hostile tactical aviation (Source: @kpszsu/63181).

Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV incursions in Sumy, Dnipropetrovsk, Chernihiv and Odesa regions, some heading toward Bilopillia and Kryvyi Rih (Source: @kpszsu/63189, @kpszsu/63171, @kpszsu/63172).
Three tankers off northern Turkey were attacked by drones; two under Sierra Leone flag, one under Palau flag, crew unharmed (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30501).
Latvia began installing anti‑tank “dragon’s teeth” on its border with Russia, part of a broader Baltic fortification effort (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30499).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian air defences downed 217 hostile drones and intercepted an Iskander‑M missile strike from Kursk. Russian forces captured villages in Dnipropetrovsk and Kharkiv regions, expanding ground control. Sweden pledged 16 Gripen fighters while Ukraine seeks additional Gripen purchases and domestic missile production licences.

points: Ukrainian air defence units, aviation and electronic warfare squads shot down 217 enemy drones of Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and other types across the country, confirming hits on 14 locations and debris falls in 7 areas (Source: @kpszsu/63176).

The Ukrainian Ministry of Defence announced plans to acquire up to 20 JAS‑39 Gripen E‑modification fighters and receive 16 older C/D variants from Sweden, with deliveries slated for early 2027 (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30505).
Budgets were increased to 1.56 trillion hryvnias and a €90 billion EU loan agreement was ratified, supporting defence spending (Source: @UkraineNow/81523).
Ukrainian forces reported guided aviation bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia region, indicating continued hostile tactical air operations (Source: @kpszsu/63181).

Multiple UAV incursions were observed: from the Black Sea toward Mykolaiv, Odesa and the Black Sea coast; from the south‑east Dnipropetrovsk toward Kryvyi Rih; and from the east toward Petropavlivka (Source: @kpszsu/63170, @kpszsu/63172, @kpszsu/63192).
Russian forces reported taking control of the villages Lesnoe and Novopodgorodne in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast and Karachne and Budarki in Kharkiv Oblast (Source: @tass_agency/378059).
A Russian Iskander‑M ballistic missile was launched from Kursk region toward Ukrainian territory during the night of 28‑29 May (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
Russian‑aligned UAVs and Shahed‑type drones were launched from multiple directions including Kursk, Orel, Bryansk and Crimea, targeting northern, southern and eastern Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
A Russian attack on a Turkish vessel traveling from Odesa to Turkey caused a fire on the superstructure; two crew members were injured and evacuated (Source: @UkraineNow/81527).
UAVs originating from the Kursk region flew eastward into Sumy Oblast, heading toward Bilopillia (Source: @kpszsu/63171).
Multiple Shahed‑type drones were reported over Sumy, with some heading toward Bilopillia and others intercepted (Source: @kpszsu/63189).
Electronic warfare and anti‑aircraft units engaged hostile drones over eastern Ukraine, reporting successful interceptions (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
Russian capture of Karachne and Budarki in Kharkiv brings additional footholds near the eastern front line (Source: @tass_agency/378059).
UAV activity was noted over Chernihiv region, including a drone near Priluk and another over the sea near the coast (Source: @eRadarrua/94114).
Four guided UAVs were launched from the Black Sea toward the city of Chornomorsk (Source: @eRadarrua/94112).
UAV sightings in the north included a group over the area of Lebedyn in Sumy and another over Batyryn in Chernihiv (Source: @eRadarrua/94085).
Russian forces reported no new territorial gains in the northern oblasts during the reporting period.
UAVs from the Black Sea moved southward into Mykolaiv and Odesa regions, indicating maritime‑origin drone threats (Source: @kpszsu/63170, @kpszsu/63168).
A guided bomb strike was recorded in Zaporizhzhia region, a key southern industrial area (Source: @kpszsu/63181).
Russian‑aligned UAVs were observed over the southern coast near the Black Sea, targeting infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/63191).
No reported ground territorial changes in the southern front during this cycle.
Ukrainian air defence shot down 217 hostile UAVs of various types across the country, demonstrating high‑intensity air‑defence activity (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aviation bombs on Zaporizhzhia, showing continued air‑strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/63181).
Swedish Gripen fighters are slated for delivery, potentially enhancing Ukraine’s air‑combat capabilities in 2027 (Source: @UkraineNow/81512).
Drone attacks on three tankers in the Black Sea near Turkey were reported, highlighting the broader maritime air‑threat environment (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30501).

key_developments: Ukrainian air defence downed 217 Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and other drones across north, south and east, while 14 locations were hit by a ballistic missile and UAVs (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
Russia captured Lesnoe and Novopodgorodne in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast and Karachne and Budarki in Kharkiv Oblast, expanding ground control (Source: @tass_agency/378059).
Sweden committed to transfer 16 JAS‑39C/D Gripen fighters to Ukraine, with deliveries expected early 2027 (Source: @UkraineNow/81512).
Guided aviation bombs were dropped on Zaporizhzhia region by hostile tactical aviation, indicating continued Russian air strikes (Source: @kpszsu/63181).
UAVs launched from the Black Sea flew toward Mykolaiv, Odesa and the Black Sea coast, showing maritime‑origin drone operations (Source: @kpszsu/63170, @kpszsu/63168, @kpszsu/63191).
Three tankers off northern Turkey were attacked by drones; two under Sierra Leone flag, one under Palau flag, crew unharmed (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30501).
Latvia began installing anti‑tank “dragon’s teeth” on its border with Russia as part of a broader Baltic fortification plan (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30499).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are intensifying combined arms operations, integrating ballistic missile strikes, tactical bombings and UAV swarms across multiple fronts. Ukrainian air defences remain effective, having intercepted over two hundred drones, but the continued use of Iskander‑M missiles and maritime‑origin UAVs poses a persistent threat to critical infrastructure. The forthcoming delivery of Swedish Gripen fighters could shift the aerial balance, yet immediate challenges stem from high‑volume drone attacks and ground incursions in Dnipropetrovsk and Kharkiv regions.
sections.regional_response: Western partners are augmenting Ukraine's defence budget and approving large‑scale financial aid, while Sweden's fighter pledge signals deeper military cooperation. Baltic states are reinforcing border defences with anti‑tank obstacles, reflecting heightened concern over potential spill‑over. Russia continues to claim territorial gains, using propaganda to justify its expanded offensive posture.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV launch points in the Black Sea and Kursk region is critical for early warning of maritime and cross‑border drone threats. The capture of villages in Dnipropetrovsk and Kharkiv may enable Russian logistics corridors, requiring focused reconnaissance. Anticipated Gripen deliveries should be integrated into air‑defence planning to maximize interoperability with existing systems.
